motivation: Add a username/password login screen that authenticates against the existing backend and gates the entire v2 Vue app behind that auth.

included: ui/src/services — the app's first HTTP client (built on ofetch) plus auth API functions (login, refresh, me, logout) against the existing /api/auth/* endpoints
ui/src/features/auth — reactive auth store (useAuth composable) with @vueuse/core useStorage session persistence and session-restore-on-reload

ui/src/features/auth/LoginView.vue — the mock's two-column signin screen (burgundy brand panel + username/password form with show/hide toggle), consuming existing DTCG semantic tokens; adds eyeoff/alert/feather/lock icons to the design-system icon set
ui/src/App.vue — auth gate that renders LoginView when unauthenticated and AppShell when authenticated
ui/src/features/shell/UserFooter.vue — show the real authenticated username and wire a logout control
ui/package.json + ui/package-lock.json — add ofetch (HTTP client) and @vueuse/core (reactive persistence); the lockfile is committed (CI runs npm ci)
excluded: OIDC / SSO login via Authelia — username/password only this binder (the mock's SSO button is dropped)
Forgot-password / reset flow and its 'sent' confirmation screen — present in the mock, excluded here
Signup / account registration ('Create an account') — the backend has no public registration endpoint (users via `make user-create`); login only
Real notes API integration — the notes list stays on mock data (mockNotes); only auth hits the real backend
Vue Router — the app stays a single gated shell; no route restructuring or router-based guards
Backend changes — /api/auth/login, /refresh, /me, /logout already exist and are unchanged
Server-side session / token revocation — logout is client-side token discard (backend JWT is stateless)

Pinia, VeeValidate/FormKit, and axios — evaluated in research and rejected for this scope: auth state stays a composable singleton, the 2-field login form stays plain controlled inputs, and ofetch covers the HTTP client more lightly than axios
jwt-decode / proactive exp-based refresh — session validation is 401- and /me-driven, not client-side-exp-driven; no JWT decoding this binder
Refactoring the existing pm_theme localStorage to useStorage — a clean follow-up, not required for login

design_facts.source: ParchMark-standalone.html (Login / signin view of the ParchMark design prototype)
design_facts.stack: Vue 3 + TypeScript + Vite frontend; custom DTCG design-token system (Style Dictionary JSON -> CSS custom properties in ui/src/design-system/tokens.css); ofetch HTTP client + @vueuse/core utilities; Vitest + @vue/test-utils; targets the existing FastAPI / JWT (HS256) backend

token_manifest.consumption_rule: semantic tier only; primitive tokens are deny-listed for new code

contract.exposes: ui/src/services/http.ts: a configured ofetch instance (ofetch.create) with baseURL from import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL (default '/api') and JSON defaults, plus setAuthHooks({ getToken, onRefresh }) for dependency injection (avoids an http<->auth import cycle). onRequest attaches 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' from getToken() when present. onResponseError: on a 401 — excluding the /auth/refresh call itself and any already-retried request — it awaits onRefresh() once and retries the original request a single time; a still-failing response throws a typed ApiError { status:number, detail:string } with detail read from the backend {detail}. ui/src/services/auth.ts (thin calls through the ofetch instance): login(username,password): Promise<{access_token,refresh_token,token_type}> -> POST /api/auth/login JSON {username,password}; refreshToken(refresh_token): Promise<TokenPair> -> POST /api/auth/refresh JSON {refresh_token}; getCurrentUser(): Promise<{username}> -> GET /api/auth/me (Bearer); logout(): Promise<void> -> POST /api/auth/logout (Bearer, best-effort).
contract.consumes: ofetch (new dependency); existing, unchanged backend endpoints under /api/auth/* (Vite already proxies /api -> http://localhost:8000).

signals: capability-escalation: introduces the app's first real network IO (the services/ HTTP layer)
capability-escalation: new dependency 'ofetch' (HTTP client) — updates package.json + package-lock.json
architectural-novelty: new services/ HTTP-client pattern entering the v2 codebase

oracle.command: npm run lint && npm run test:coverage
assertions: login() POSTs JSON {username,password} to /api/auth/login and returns {access_token,refresh_token,token_type}
the ofetch instance attaches 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' from the injected getToken() when a token is present, and omits it when none

a 401 triggers the injected onRefresh() once and retries the original request a single time; a still-failing retry throws ApiError with the backend {detail} and HTTP status (the /auth/refresh call itself is never retried)
baseURL comes from VITE_API_URL (default '/api'); getCurrentUser() GETs /api/auth/me with the bearer header
lint (vue-tsc + prettier) and the vitest suite pass at the >=90% coverage floor

work_items.title: Auth state store (useAuth composable) + session persistence

contract.exposes: ui/src/features/auth/useAuth.ts: a module-singleton composable. Session state (access token, refresh token, user) is persisted with @vueuse/core useStorage under key 'pm_auth'. Exposes reactive user: Ref<{username}|null>, isAuthenticated: ComputedRef<boolean>, error: Ref<string|null>, pending: Ref<boolean>. On module init it registers http.ts setAuthHooks({ getToken: () => the stored access token, onRefresh: the deduped refresh below }). Actions: login(username,password): Promise<boolean> (services/auth.login, store tokens + user; set error from ApiError.detail on failure), logout(): Promise<void> (best-effort services/auth.logout, then clear state + storage), restoreSession(): Promise<void> (on load: if a stored token exists, validate via services/auth.getCurrentUser — set user on 200, clear session on 401), refresh(): Promise<boolean> (services/auth.refreshToken, stores the new pair; concurrent callers share one in-flight request via a module-level refreshPromise).
contract.consumes: ui/src/services/auth.ts (login, getCurrentUser, logout, refreshToken); ui/src/services/http.ts (setAuthHooks); @vueuse/core useStorage (new dependency).

signals: sensitive-zone: holds auth tokens and session state, persisted in localStorage
capability-escalation: token/session persistence via @vueuse/core useStorage (new dependency '@vueuse/core' — updates package.json + package-lock.json)
architectural-novelty: first app-wide shared-state store (useAuth composable singleton) in v2

oracle.command: npm run lint && npm run test:coverage
assertions: login() success sets isAuthenticated true and persists user + tokens via @vueuse/core useStorage under key 'pm_auth'
logout() clears user, isAuthenticated, and the 'pm_auth' storage entry
restoreSession() with a valid stored token sets user via /api/auth/me; a 401 clears the stored session
concurrent refresh() callers share a single in-flight /api/auth/refresh request (no duplicate refresh calls)
a failed login leaves isAuthenticated false and sets error from the backend detail
lint and the vitest suite pass at the >=90% coverage floor

work_items.title: Login screen (LoginView.vue) — ports the ParchMark mock signin view

component_map.note: two-column grid (1.05fr brand aside / 1fr form), full-height; collapses to one column (form only) below the mobile breakpoint

burgundy-gradient panel with paper-grain dot texture; serif 'ParchMark' wordmark ('Mark' tinted), italic tagline 'A calm home for your markdown.', a serif pull-quote, and 3 feature rows (eye / feather / lock icons); hidden on mobile
centered card (max 400px) on --canvas; serif h1 'Welcome back', sub 'Sign in to pick up where you left off.'
controlled <input> v-model, label 'Username', placeholder 'jamie', autocomplete username; focus-ring box-shadow 0 0 0 3px var(--focus-ring)
controlled <input> whose type toggles password/text via a show/hide button (eye / eyeoff icons), label 'Password', autocomplete current-password
full-width, filled --accent, label 'Sign in'; shows an inline spinner and disables while a login request is in flight
--danger text on --danger-surface with an alert icon; bound to useAuth().error

icon_map.source: new — port the mock's eye-off SVG via the createIcon factory in ui/src/design-system/icons/index.ts
new — port the mock's alert/triangle SVG via createIcon
new — port the mock's feather SVG via createIcon
new — port the mock's lock SVG via createIcon

token_changes.resolution: inlined in LoginView scoped CSS, mirroring the mock's .auth-aside — no semantic match exists and the brand panel is the only consumer; build may instead promote it to an additive --auth-brand-* semantic token if preferred
token_changes.auth: same in light + dark (brand panel is dark in both themes)

token_changes.resolution: the mock's #b3261e / #fdecea map exactly to the existing semantic tokens; no addition

contract.exposes: ui/src/features/auth/LoginView.vue — ports the mock's signin view: a two-column auth-shell with a burgundy-gradient brand aside (wordmark + tagline + pull-quote + 3 feature rows; auto-hidden on mobile) and a form card. The form: serif 'Welcome back' h1, sub copy, a Username field (placeholder 'jamie'), a Password field with a show/hide toggle, an inline error region bound to useAuth().error, and a full-width 'Sign in' submit that shows a spinner and disables while pending. On submit it calls useAuth().login(username,password). Controlled inputs follow the SearchBox.vue idiom; structural color/shape come from existing semantic tokens, with the brand-panel gradient inlined as a documented one-off. Explicitly excludes the mock's SSO button, 'or' divider, 'Forgot?' link, and 'Create an account' switch.
contract.consumes: ui/src/features/auth/useAuth.ts (login action, error + pending refs); existing semantic tokens from ui/src/design-system/tokens.css; ui/src/design-system/icons (eye, plus new eyeoff/alert/feather/lock).

signals: primary user-facing deliverable of this binder
blast-radius: adds 4 icons (eyeoff, alert, feather, lock) to the shared design-system icon set (ui/src/design-system/icons/index.ts)

oracle.command: npm run lint && npm run test:coverage
assertions: renders the brand aside (wordmark + feature rows) and a form card with a username input, a password input, and a 'Sign in' submit
the password show/hide toggle flips the input between type=password and type=text
submitting the form calls useAuth().login with the entered username and password
when login rejects, the backend error detail is rendered in the error region
inputs and submit button are disabled while a login request is in flight
no SSO button, 'Forgot?' link, or 'Create an account' switch is rendered (out of scope)
structural colors use semantic tokens; the only literal colors are the brand-panel gradient stops, mirroring the mock's .auth-aside (reviewer-checked against the diff)
lint and the vitest suite pass at the >=90% coverage floor

work_items.title: Gate the app shell behind auth + wire logout

component_map.note: calls useAuth().restoreSession() on mount with a minimal loading state, then renders <LoginView/> when !isAuthenticated else <AppShell/>
bind useAuth().user.username in place of the hardcoded 'Jamie Doe'; add a logout control that calls useAuth().logout()

contract.exposes: ui/src/App.vue gates the whole app: on mount it awaits useAuth().restoreSession() (showing a minimal loading state until it resolves), then renders <LoginView/> when !isAuthenticated and <AppShell/> when authenticated. ui/src/features/shell/UserFooter.vue replaces the hardcoded 'Jamie Doe' identity with useAuth().user.username and exposes a logout control that calls useAuth().logout(), returning the user to LoginView via the gate.
contract.consumes: ui/src/features/auth/useAuth.ts (isAuthenticated, user, restoreSession, logout); ui/src/features/auth/LoginView.vue; ui/src/features/shell/AppShell.vue (unchanged in behavior).

signals: blast-radius: rewires App.vue (the app entrypoint) so the entire app renders behind the auth gate
contract-mutation: changes existing App.vue + UserFooter render behavior and must update the existing App.test.ts to the gated behavior

oracle.command: npm run lint && npm run test:coverage
assertions: App renders LoginView when useAuth reports unauthenticated
App renders AppShell when authenticated
App calls restoreSession() on mount
UserFooter shows the authenticated user's username and its logout control calls useAuth().logout()
the pre-existing App shell test is updated to the gated behavior and the full vitest suite stays green at the >=90% coverage floor
